findings.reasoning: This page (turbotax.intuit.com/reviews/online/free-edition) is a customer reviews page for TurboTax Free Edition tax-filing software. The page discusses tax preparation offers, guarantees, pricing, Refund Advance loans, File Now Pay Later loans, and Credit Karma Money accounts. None of these are the deposit product itself being advertised as the primary subject of the page in a way that requires FDIC insurance language on THIS page for the deposit account itself — the page does mention 'Credit Karma Money™ Account' and 'MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC' in the context of the Refund Advance/5-days-early disclosures, and 'Router Account' at an 'FDIC insured bank' for File Now Pay Later. These are ancillary loan-related banking disclosures embedded in footnotes about other products (Refund Advance loan, File Now Pay Later loan), not primary advertising of a deposit product like Credit Karma Money Spend/Save account. The rule R-03 is specifically about pages advertising a deposit product requiring 'FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]' language. Since this page's core subject is TurboTax Free Edition (a tax software product, not a deposit product), and the deposit-related banking mentions are incidental within loan disclosures (mentioning Member FDIC status of a bank generally, not making a deposit-insurance claim about a specific deposit account up to $250,000), the trigger for R-03 is not met. Strongest counter-reading: one could argue the mentions of 'Credit Karma Money™ checking account,' 'MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC,' and Router Account at 'FDIC insured bank' constitute deposit product advertising triggering R-03. However, these are passing mentions of the FDIC membership status of banks facilitating other products (refund early deposit feature, tax payment loan), not an advertisement of a deposit product itself with its own insurance claim requiring the '$250,000 through [Bank]' formulation. No text approaches the specific $250,000 insured amount formulation anywhere on this page, and the primary advertised product is the Free Edition software, not a deposit account. Therefore not_applicable.

Rule R-03 applies only when a deposit product is being advertised and therefore FDIC insurance language is required. This page is focused on TurboTax Free Edition reviews, online filing offers, guarantees, and loan products such as TurboTax Refund Advance and File Now, Pay Later. The only banking-related mentions are to Credit Karma Money accounts and an FDIC-insured router account, but those are in the context of funds movement and loan disbursement, not as an advertised deposit product of Intuit or a partner bank. No specific deposit account product is being marketed on this page that would trigger the requirement to include the specified FDIC insurance language. Therefore, the rule is untriggered and marked not_applicable.

findings.evidence_quote: Get your tax refund up to 5 days early in a Credit Karma Money™ Account: When it’s time to file, have your tax refund direct deposited to a Credit Karma Money™ checking account, and you could receive your funds up to 5 days early. If you choose to pay your tax preparation fee with TurboTax using your federal tax refund or if you choose to take the TurboTax Refund Advance loan, you will not be eligible to receive your refund up to 5 days early with Credit Karma. 5-day early program may change or discontinue at any time. Up to 5 days early access to your federal tax refund is compared to standard tax refund electronic deposit and is dependent on and subject to IRS submitting refund information to the bank before release date. IRS may not submit refund information early. Excludes business tax returns. Credit Karma is not a bank. Banking services for Credit Karma Money accounts are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC. Maximum balance and transfer limits apply per account.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/MOBILE section – bullet about “Get your tax refund up to 5 days early in a Credit Karma Money Account”
findings.reasoning: Trigger: The page promotes/use of a deposit product (Credit Karma Money checking account) and names the bank (MVB Bank). Requirement: R-03 demands explicit FDIC insurance language stating the deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through the named bank. The page only says “Member FDIC” and does not state coverage up to $250,000, so Axis A is not met. Because the canonical approved phrasing is not used, Axis B also fails. Strongest counter-reading: One could argue this is a tax software page and only incidentally references banking; however, the copy actively promotes using a Credit Karma Money checking account feature (early access), which constitutes advertising a deposit product, triggering the rule.

findings.evidence_quote: Additional requirements: You must (a) e-file your federal tax return with TurboTax and (b) currently have or open a Credit Karma Money™ Spend (checking) account with MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC. Maximum balance and transfer limits apply. Opening a Credit Karma Money™ Spend (checking) account is subject to eligibility. Please see Credit Karma Money Spend Account Terms and Disclosures for details.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/MOBILE section – Refund Advance program, “Additional requirements” paragraph
findings.reasoning: Trigger: This language encourages consumers to have or open a Credit Karma Money Spend (checking) account (a deposit product) with a named bank. Requirement: R-03 requires the FDIC statement to explicitly say the deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through the bank. The text only includes “Member FDIC” and omits the $250,000 coverage language and the required construction, so Axis A fails. The approved phrasing is not used, so Axis B fails. Counter-reading: One might say this is merely an eligibility condition for a loan rather than advertising the deposit account; however, it still promotes opening/holding the checking account with the bank, so the rule is triggered.
